title: potentilla recta subsp. afra
intro: Potentilla recta subsp. afra is a subspecies of the Potentilla recta plant, commonly known as the Sulphur Cinquefoil, which is a perennial herb in the rose family (Rosaceae) with yellow flowers and a strong, pungent smell. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and treat various gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia. The health benefits of Potentilla recta subsp. afra include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The plant is typically prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, and can also be used in the form of tincture or ointment, although its strong scent may require caution when handling.
